commit.message:
Android perf: setPixelRatio, rAF-gate pointermove, anchor guard

- scrib_renderer.ts: renderer.setPixelRatio(Math.min(devicePixelRatio, 2))
  Caps the WebGL framebuffer at 2× CSS pixels. On POCO 5 (DPR ≈ 2.75) the
  previous DPR=1 framebuffer was upscaled blurrily by the OS compositor;
  DPR=2 is sharp and roughly 2× cheaper than native resolution.

- main_app.ts: rAF-gate pointermove handler
  The event handler now stashes the latest PointerEvent and schedules one
  requestAnimationFrame. The rAF callback drains the event using
  editor.pointermove() (which already uses getCoalescedEvents() for
  stroke fidelity). At 120 Hz touch rate this reduces main-thread
  pixel-work scheduling from 120×/frame to 1×/frame.

- main_app.ts: _redraw_anchor_canvas early-return when no anchors
  Previously, every pointermove triggered a full getImageData + putImageData
  on the doc-sized anchor canvas even when no anchors existed. Now the
  function returns immediately if anchor_manager.anchors.length === 0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Mobile performance: viewport meta, camera cache, setSize, coalesced events, narrow getImageData

1. index.html: add <meta name=viewport> — prevents 980px virtual viewport
   on Android; fixes layout, touch coords, and default zoom behaviour.

2. scrib_renderer.ts: cache OrthographicCamera — previously init_camera()
   allocated a new camera object every RAF call. Now one camera is created
   at startup; update_camera() mutates its frustum + calls updateProjectionMatrix()
   and renderer.setSize() so Three.js stays in sync with canvas resizes.

3. editor.ts: use getCoalescedEvents() in pointermove — Android fires
   60–120 touch samples between frames; all were processed sequentially per
   event, blowing the frame budget. Now all coalesced positions are fed to
   tool.drag() for correct stroke coverage, but rendering stays throttled
   to one RAF.

4. scribble.ts: narrow getImageData to stroke segment bbox — previously the
   full tool canvas (= full document) was read and written on every pointer
   move. Now only a tight bounding box (segment endpoints ± radius + 1px)
   is transferred. For a 500px document this reduces the pixel transfer from
   250k to ~(2r+3)² per event (~hundreds vs millions of bytes).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(acquirer): cover acquire_git clone-failure and git_ref branches

Add two unit tests that exercise genuinely uncovered branches in
acquire_git and run_git_clone by attempting to clone an invalid URL:

- acquire_git_invalid_url_returns_error: covers the True branch of
  'if let Err(e) = clone_result' (line 121) and the False branch of
  'if let Some(r) = git_ref' (line 174), plus the failure arm of
  'if output.status.success()' (line 185) in run_git_clone.

- acquire_git_with_ref_invalid_url_returns_error: covers the True branch
  of 'if let Some(r) = git_ref' (line 174) by passing a git_ref value.

Both tests still fail at the clone step (invalid URL), so no real
network access or git server is required; git is present in the CI

fix: restore NPM_TOKEN auth and add --access public for scoped package publish (#7475)

* fix: explicitly dispatch CI after tag push to build release binaries

GITHUB_TOKEN pushes do not trigger new workflow runs (GitHub prevents
recursive loop triggers). This means the tag push from the release
workflow never triggered osrm-backend.yml, so no binaries were built
or uploaded to the release assets.

Changes:
- Add workflow_dispatch trigger to osrm-backend.yml
- Add 'actions: write' permission to release workflow
- After pushing the tag, explicitly dispatch osrm-backend.yml on the
  tag ref; CI runs with GITHUB_REF=refs/tags/v* so PUBLISH=On and
  prebuilt binaries are built and uploaded
- Restore NODE_AUTH_TOKEN and add --access public for scoped npm package

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove redundant NPM_TOKEN and --access public from publish step

publishConfig.access=public is already set in package.json, so --access
public is redundant. OIDC via setup-node handles authentication when
id-token: write is set.

commit.message:
fix: stabilize flaky Fluent UI Dialog portal tests

Replace userEvent.click() with fireEvent.click() for dialog-trigger
clicks in agentConfigCard, templateCard, and settingsPanel tests.

Root cause: userEvent simulates a full pointer/mouse/focus event
sequence that doesn't reliably trigger React state updates for
dialog-opening clicks under parallel test load in jsdom. The
multi-event simulation creates timing windows where the Portal
mount can be delayed past the findByRole timeout.

fireEvent.click() dispatches a single synchronous click event,
which deterministically triggers the onClick handler and React
state update. Dialog content interactions (clicking buttons within
the dialog) still use userEvent.click() since the portal is already
mounted by that point.

Also: use within(dialog).getByRole() to scope button queries to
the dialog portal, and configure asyncUtilTimeout for safety margin.

Verified: 20/20 consecutive full-suite runs (1835 tests each) pass.
Previously failed ~30% of runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
